flavor_text: Charred at the tip from cheap practice still points true when the hand stops shaking.
Plain wood and patient balance teach restraint before power.
Pinprick burns in glove leather teach control by small losses.
Ash packed rings keep the hands from slipping, heat is less a threat than haste.
Cloudy glass catches lantern light and makes a poor room feel less hopeless.
Tapped wood hums back, a strict tutor for rushed cadence.
Shallow grooves guide the fingers when the mind wanders.
Wax and cold stone smell like work done past sleep.
Cracked varnish like dried riverbeds, it survives careless travel and careful use.
Silver flecks under lacquer hold a tiny sky. Ugly nights go quieter.
Made for spoken rites and steady breath. The voice breaks less.
A smudged mark refuses to wash away. Mistakes leave evidence.
Dark wrap and blunt cap, it keeps work contained when fear wants it wild.
Squared and heavy in the hand prefers clean decisions. Wavering costs more.
Cool metal teaches patience the hard way, pride finds no room.
Bound tight at the head, the mind scatters less when sleep is thin.
Ceremonial shape and a worn grip have seen more repetition than praise.
A narrow channel runs true along the shaft. Calm hands are rewarded, drift is exposed.
A dried bloom under glass remembers decay. Discipline stays.
Tall and severe, even bold students stand straighter and speak less.
Ash dark in the grip and steady in the air asks for composure, not courage.
Dark and singing in the grip turns fear into a held note. Silence follows.
Ringed crown and clockwork patience keep the mind from chasing shadows.
Glass and waterline stains carry weight like a vow. Match it.
Imposing and deliberate, it dares you to hold the line when the room goes silent.
